title: saxifraga rivularis subsp. hyperborea
intro: Saxifraga rivularis subsp. hyperborea, also known as the Arctic saxifrage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the saxifrage family, native to the Arctic regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Saxifraga rivularis subsp. hyperborea include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as having antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tea or tincture, and it has also been used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments such as digestive issues and skin conditions.
